build.determinism_note: Scope N2-A.1: --disable-build-servers added after workflow run 29371996936 found an intermittent producer-ordering defect (Roslyn shared-compiler-server scheduling caused CoreCompile diagnostics to interleave in different orders across independent builds, though the full warning multiset, exit code, and every other semantic field always matched). Proven deterministic across 6/6 repeated fresh builds on real ubuntu-24.04 GitHub-hosted runners via qodec/evals/interop/v2/n2/canary/tools/determinism_probe.py — see n2a-determinism-probe-report.json. -p:UseSharedCompilation=false alone was tested and found insufficient (1/6 divergence); --disable-build-servers alone was sufficient and is the minimal change.

notes: Selected manually by explicit user approval outside this session's automated discovery (N2-A has no RepoSelector/CandidateScorer).
Do not substitute another revision or repository if this exact commit becomes unreachable — stop and report instead (see qodec-n2-miner-canary.yml fallback job).
